name: Bharatiya Janata Party
text: BJP is the ruling party at the centre. Founded in April 1980 by Atal Bihari Vajpayee and L.K. Advani, emerging from the Bharatiya Jana Sangh (BJS) after the collapse of the Janata Party. President: J.P. Nadda. Ideology: Hindutva, Integral Humanism, Cultural Nationalism. Part of NDA. Won 240 seats in 2024 Lok Sabha (303 in 2019, 282 in 2014). Key leaders: Narendra Modi, Amit Shah, Rajnath Singh, Yogi Adityanath.

name: Indian National Congress
text: Congress (INC) is one of the oldest political parties in India, founded in December 1885 by Allan Octavian Hume, Dadabhai Naoroji, and Dinshaw Wacha. It led the Indian independence movement. President: Mallikarjun Kharge. Key leaders: Rahul Gandhi (Leader of Opposition), Sonia Gandhi, Priyanka Gandhi. Won 99 seats in 2024 Lok Sabha. Part of INDIA alliance.

name: Aam Aadmi Party
text: AAP was founded by Arvind Kejriwal in 2012 after the Anna Hazare anti-corruption movement. Governs Punjab (CM: Bhagwant Mann). Won 3 seats in 2024 Lok Sabha. Known for focus on education, healthcare, free water and electricity. National party since 2023.

name: All India Trinamool Congress
text: TMC was founded by Mamata Banerjee in 1998 after splitting from Congress. Rules West Bengal since 2011. Won 29 seats in 2024 Lok Sabha. Key leaders: Mamata Banerjee, Abhishek Banerjee. Part of INDIA alliance.

name: Bahujan Samaj Party
text: BSP was founded by Kanshi Ram in 1984, currently led by Mayawati. Represents Dalit and backward communities. Mayawati served 4 times as UP CM. Party's influence has declined in recent elections. Won 0 seats in 2024 Lok Sabha.

name: Samajwadi Party
text: SP was founded by Mulayam Singh Yadav in 1992. Currently led by son Akhilesh Yadav. Won 37 seats in 2024 Lok Sabha, a massive jump. Key base: Yadav and Muslim communities in UP. Part of INDIA alliance.

name: Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam
text: DMK was founded by C. N. Annadurai in 1949. Current president: M.K. Stalin (CM of Tamil Nadu). Won 22 seats in 2024 Lok Sabha. Dominant political force in Tamil Nadu. Part of INDIA alliance and a key ally of Congress.

name: Telugu Desam Party
text: TDP was founded by N.T. Rama Rao in 1982. Current president: Chandrababu Naidu (CM of Andhra Pradesh since 2024). Won 16 seats in 2024 Lok Sabha. Key NDA ally. Known for IT development in Hyderabad.

name: Janata Dal (United)
text: JD(U) is led by Bihar CM Nitish Kumar. Won 12 seats in 2024 Lok Sabha. Key NDA ally. Nitish Kumar has switched between NDA and opposition multiple times. Base primarily in Bihar.

name: Shiv Sena
president: Eknath Shinde faction recognized by EC
text: Shiv Sena was founded by Bal Thackeray in 1966. Split in 2022 into Eknath Shinde faction (NDA) and Uddhav Thackeray faction (Sena-UBT, INDIA alliance). Shinde faction won 7 seats, UBT won 9 seats in 2024.

name: Nationalist Congress Party
text: NCP founded by Sharad Pawar in 1999 after splitting from Congress. Split again in 2023: Ajit Pawar faction joined NDA, Sharad Pawar faction (NCP-SP) is with INDIA alliance. Sharad Pawar's faction won 8 seats in 2024 Lok Sabha.

name: Communist Party of India (Marxist)
president: Sitaram Yechury (late) / New Gen Sec TBD
text: CPI(M) is the largest communist party in India. Rules Kerala (CM: Pinarayi Vijayan). Won 4 seats in 2024 Lok Sabha. Part of Left Democratic Front in Kerala and Left Front in West Bengal. Key cadre-based party.

name: Rashtriya Janata Dal
text: RJD was founded by Lalu Prasad Yadav in 1997 after splitting from Janata Dal. Key leader: Tejashwi Yadav (son of Lalu). Base primarily in Bihar among Yadav and Muslim communities. Won 4 seats in 2024 Lok Sabha. Part of INDIA alliance.
